report_name: European Parliament Member Investigating Spyware Was Hacked with Pegasus

threat_actors.name: NSO Group
threat_actors.description: Developer and vendor of the Pegasus spyware, which is sold exclusively to government clients. The company's software was used to hack MEP Stelios Kouloglou.
threat_actors.goals: Commercial sale of surveillance technology to government clients for intelligence gathering purposes.

threat_actors.name: Unnamed Pegasus Operator
threat_actors.description: A government client of NSO Group that operated the Pegasus spyware to infect MEP Stelios Kouloglou. Technical indicators link this operator to a known customer that has previously targeted Russian and Belarusian-speaking journalists in Europe.
threat_actors.goals: To conduct surveillance on a member of the European Parliament's PEGA committee, likely to compromise confidential parliamentary work.

attack_patterns.name: Exploitation for Initial Access
attack_patterns.description: The attack used a zero-click exploit, likely delivered via a silent push message, to gain initial access to the target's iPhone. The 'PWNYOURHOME' exploit targeting Apple's HomeKit framework was used in one of the infections.

attack_patterns.name: Exploitation for Privilege Escalation
attack_patterns.description: Pegasus spyware achieves root-level access to the compromised mobile device, granting it complete control over the operating system.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Local System
attack_patterns.description: Once installed, Pegasus can access all data on the device, including encrypted messages from applications like Signal and WhatsApp, emails, photos, and contacts.

attack_patterns.name: Audio Capture
attack_patterns.description: The Pegasus spyware can activate the device's microphone for live surveillance of conversations and the surrounding environment.

attack_patterns.name: Video Capture
attack_patterns.description: The Pegasus spyware can activate the device's camera to capture video for live surveillance.

malware.name: Pegasus
malware.description: A sophisticated mobile surveillance tool developed by NSO Group, used to infect the iPhone of MEP Stelios Kouloglou. It is capable of zero-click infection, full device compromise, and comprehensive data exfiltration.

vulnerabilities.name: PWNYOURHOME
vulnerabilities.description: A zero-click exploit targeting a vulnerability in Apple's HomeKit framework, used by the Pegasus spyware to infect the target's iPhone in October 2022.

identities.name: Stelios Kouloglou
identities.description: A former Greek Member of the European Parliament (MEP) and member of the PEGA committee investigating spyware abuse, who was targeted and hacked with Pegasus spyware.

identities.name: European Parliament
identities.description: A democratic institution of the European Union whose PEGA investigative committee was targeted through the hacking of one of its members, Stelios Kouloglou.

identities.name: NSO Group
identities.description: An Israeli technology firm that develops and sells the Pegasus spyware exclusively to government clients.

infrastructure.name: Pegasus C2 Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Command and control servers used by the Pegasus spyware to exfiltrate data and receive commands. The specific operator's infrastructure was noted to have been used in other attacks against journalists in Europe.
